location.office_time_required: up to 10% travel for onboarding, team meetings, and industry events

level_signals: Owns vision and roadmap independently; drives cross-functional initiatives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7–10+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or data tooling.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or data tooling

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong technical depth with the ability to work closely with engineers on system design and architecture decisions.
Experience working with data governance frameworks, metadata systems, semantic layers, or analytics infrastructure.
Comfortable operating in highly technical environments involving large-scale data systems or AI/ML platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to define clear metrics, KPIs, and system performance indicators.
Experience evaluating build vs. buy decisions for complex infrastructure products.
Excellent cross-functional communicator who can align engineering, data, and product stakeholders around a shared strategy.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: An engineering or technical background is highly valued but not required.

scope.surface: Data Management and Governance capabilities across Klaviyo's data platform
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / marketing automation platform (ecommerce focus)
core_responsibilities: Define and own the product vision and roadmap for Klaviyo's data management and governance capabilities.
Partner closely with engineering and data platform teams to design scalable systems for data governance, metadata management, lineage, and data quality.
Drive the development of semantic data layers and modeling frameworks that simplify how teams interact with complex data systems.
Evaluate and lead build vs. buy decisions for components of the data management stack.
Collaborate with AI/ML teams to ensure data systems support emerging AI-powered and agent-based capabilities.
Define and track success metrics around data quality, governance adoption, discoverability, and platform reliability.
Lead cross-functional initiatives that improve how data is structured, documented, and accessed across the organization.
Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, and technical decisions clearly across engineering, leadership, and cross-functional partners.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package alongside annual cash bonus and sign-on payments

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($168K–$252K base) with equity and bonus mentioned
Clear product surface and ownership area (Data Management and Governance)
Engineering background 'highly valued but not required' — reduces gatekeeping
Role sits at strategic intersection of data platform, analytics, and AI
red_flags: 7–10+ years minimum is a high bar; combined with Lead title, narrows the pool significantly
No city listed — location type and remote eligibility are partially ambiguous; only US geographic pay range is referenced
Use of Covey AEDT tool for hiring screening (noted specifically for NYC candidates) may introduce bias concerns
Travel requirement mentioned but framed loosely as 'up to 10%'

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, title, and years requirement)

location.type (JD body does not explicitly state hybrid; inferred from travel requirement language and US-wide pay range suggesting distributed/remote-eligible workforce)

ambiguities: Location type not explicitly stated in JD body. The pay range covers 'various U.S. geographic markets' and no specific city is required, suggesting remote-US eligible, but the term 'hybrid' or 'remote' is never used directly.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not addressed anywhere in this JD.
Title in URL/metadata is 'Lead Product Manager, Data Management' but JD body header says 'Lead Product Manager, Data Management/Governance' — treated as the same role.
